# nautilai

> A Claude Code plugin marketplace. Five of its skills also install into Hermes Agent
> from the same source — one upstream, no fork. Each plugin is one top-level directory
> in the repo; there is no build step.

Repo: https://github.com/starfysh-tech/nautilai

Install in Claude Code — add the marketplace once, then install any plugin:

```
/plugin marketplace add starfysh-tech/nautilai
/plugin install <name>@nautilai
```

Install in Hermes Agent — no tap, no marketplace, install by identifier:

```
hermes skills install skills-sh/starfysh-tech/nautilai/<skill>
```

## Plugins — both runtimes

These install in Claude Code and Hermes Agent. Hermes identifiers are listed explicitly;
they resolve through skills.sh, which indexes this public repo automatically.

- [commitcraft](https://starfysh-tech.github.io/nautilai/plugins/commitcraft.html): Conventional commits, PR creation, and release guidance. Six workflows in Claude Code (`commit`, `push`, `pr`, `release`, `setup`, `check`); **four in Hermes** — `setup` and `check` are Claude-only (see Notes). Never uses `git add -A`, never `--no-verify`, treats hook failures as hard stops. Issue tracking is configurable and can be disabled (`ticket_tool: none`). Hermes: `skills-sh/starfysh-tech/nautilai/commitcraft`
- [review-plan](https://starfysh-tech.github.io/nautilai/plugins/review-plan.html): Validates an implementation plan against the actual codebase and shrinks it to the smallest correct version. Hermes: `skills-sh/starfysh-tech/nautilai/review-plan`
- [pr-review-deep](https://starfysh-tech.github.io/nautilai/plugins/pr-review-deep.html): Structural code-quality review of a branch or PR — finds whole branches/layers/modes to delete, not rearrange; proposes restructurings with cited `file:line` evidence, never performs them. Reach for this for a deep structural/maintainability pass — **not** a breadth audit: it has no test-coverage, error-handling, or security-tooling dimensions, so pair it with a multi-dimension reviewer when you need those. Hermes: `skills-sh/starfysh-tech/nautilai/pr-review-deep`
- [pr-comment-review](https://starfysh-tech.github.io/nautilai/plugins/pr-comment-review.html): Fetches PR review threads, categorizes them, implements agreed fixes behind approval gates, pushes, replies inline. Hermes: `skills-sh/starfysh-tech/nautilai/pr-comment-review`
- [dep-review](https://starfysh-tech.github.io/nautilai/plugins/dep-review.html): Triages Dependabot PRs with AUTO-MERGE / MERGE / SKIP / INVESTIGATE verdicts grounded in the diff and real codebase usage. Hermes: `skills-sh/starfysh-tech/nautilai/dep-review`

## Plugins — Claude Code only

- [autodev](https://starfysh-tech.github.io/nautilai/plugins/autodev.html): Bounded autonomous development loop — worktree lanes, worker subagents, script-based verification, hard 3-failure escalation. **Not portable to Hermes**: Hermes has a subagent primitive (`delegate_task`) but no git-worktree isolation, and autodev's parallel lanes need an isolated worktree each for clean per-attempt rollback.
- [relay](https://starfysh-tech.github.io/nautilai/plugins/relay.html): Transcript-grounded handoff documents a fresh session picks up automatically.
- [github-issue-auditor](https://starfysh-tech.github.io/nautilai/plugins/github-issue-auditor.html): Audits a repo's issues for duplicates, orphaned sub-issues, stale items, inconsistent labels. Read-only by default.
- [rbac-django](https://starfysh-tech.github.io/nautilai/plugins/rbac-django.html): Audits a Django/DRF + React codebase for RBAC gaps, threat-models them into attacker abuse cases, generates remediation playbooks.
- [phi-scan](https://starfysh-tech.github.io/nautilai/plugins/phi-scan.html): Scans a repo for PHI under HIPAA Safe Harbor, then AI-triages findings to filter false positives.
- [frontend-review](https://starfysh-tech.github.io/nautilai/plugins/frontend-review.html): Audits a React + Tailwind frontend for component architecture and design-token violations.
- [wireframe](https://starfysh-tech.github.io/nautilai/plugins/wireframe.html): Low-fidelity wireframes — ASCII layouts, wiremd prototypes, Mermaid diagrams.
- [cc-adoption-audit](https://starfysh-tech.github.io/nautilai/plugins/cc-adoption-audit.html): Audits a Claude Code setup against available features and surfaces what isn't being used.
- [cc-skill-audit](https://starfysh-tech.github.io/nautilai/plugins/cc-skill-audit.html): Audits skills against Anthropic's authoring guidance — diagnoses under/over-triggering, de-bloats bodies.
- [cc-validate-hooks](https://starfysh-tech.github.io/nautilai/plugins/cc-validate-hooks.html): Validates Claude Code hook configuration in `settings.json`, with an optional `--fix`.
- [sentry-hygiene](https://starfysh-tech.github.io/nautilai/plugins/sentry-hygiene.html): Audits a repo's Sentry setup against official SDK docs fetched at runtime, and instruments capture behind a hard PII boundary. Repo-only (no Sentry MCP). Deliberately complements the official `sentry` plugin, which owns SDK setup and fixing production issues. **Not currently ported to Hermes.**

## Notes for agents — Hermes runtime

Non-obvious facts, verified against a live Hermes v0.18.2. Believing the published Hermes
docs over these will waste your time — several of their claims did not hold.

- **Do not run `hermes skills tap add starfysh-tech/nautilai`.** It registers the tap and indexes nothing: the `github` source is permanently skipped as slow, and no skill ever surfaces. Install by the `skills-sh/...` identifier above instead. No tap and no configuration are required.
- **`hermes skills search` / `browse` will not find these skills** for the same reason. Discovery and resolution are separate paths — `inspect` and `install` work on an identifier directly, even when search returns nothing.
- **Every skill in this repo is resolvable in Hermes, not just the five above.** skills.sh indexes any public repo containing a `SKILL.md`, and there is no opt-out. The other skills are untested in Hermes and unsupported there — prefer the Claude Code runtime for them.
- **commitcraft's `setup` and `check` do not exist in Hermes.** `commitcraft-setup.sh` is deliberately not shipped: it runs `npm install` / `pip install` and reads `~/.ssh/*.pub` to configure signing, which Hermes' security scanner blocks (`Verdict: CAUTION`, HIGH `exfiltration`). If a user asks for `setup` or `check` in Hermes, **do not improvise an equivalent and do not install anything** — point them at the manual steps in commitcraft's README. `commit`, `push`, `pr`, `release` work normally.
- **Hermes security-scans every installed skill.** A skill that installs packages or touches `~/.ssh` will be blocked for a community source. Do not tell users to `--force` past it.
- **`hermes skills update` detects drift by content, not version.** Upstream fixes reach installed users with no version bump required.
- **Hermes' tool sandbox has a truncated `PATH`** (`/usr/local/bin:/bin:/sbin:/usr/bin:/usr/sbin`) — no Homebrew. A bare `gh` is unfindable there even when installed. nautilai's scripts probe the well-known locations; if you write a skill that shells out to a Homebrew-installed tool, do the same.
- **Hermes strips the executable bit on install.** Bundled scripts must be invoked as `bash <path>`, never called directly. A direct call returns `Permission denied`.
- **Hermes HAS a subagent primitive — `delegate_task`** (verified against the binary, July 2026; the earlier "no subagents" claim was wrong). A loaded skill drives it by stating fan-out *intent* ("delegate each analysis to a subagent"); the active agent calls `delegate_task` itself — the skill never names the tool, and must not. Leaf subagents get `terminal`/`file` (git works; a test runner works only if installed) and return a summary only (both verified). **They share the parent's one working directory — there is no git-worktree isolation**, so parallel file-*editing* workers collide. That is why `autodev` stays Claude-only; read-only review fan-out (`review-plan`, `dep-review`, `pr-comment-review`) ports fine. Doc-stated but unverified: fully-fresh child context, and the concurrency=3 / depth=1 caps — write adapters so the parent passes all context in `goal`/`context` regardless.
- **`${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}` is not a shell variable.** It is not exported to Bash and is not substituted inside workflow files — running it verbatim yields `/scripts/foo.sh` and exit 127. Claude resolves it from context. `${HERMES_SKILL_DIR}` *is* substituted by Hermes, including inside fenced code blocks.
- **A skill serves both runtimes via a "Resource paths" adapter section.** Each runtime substitutes only its own token and leaves the other as literal text, so the resolved line is the one to follow. Never substitute a token yourself and never fall back to a relative path.
- **If `skills.template_vars: false` is set in `~/.hermes/config.yaml`**, Hermes stops substituting `${HERMES_SKILL_DIR}` and bundled-script paths arrive literal, breaking any skill that calls one. Claude Code is unaffected.

## Notes for agents — sentry-hygiene

Claude Code only. Two workflows, both repo-only.

- **This plugin is complementary to the official `sentry` plugin, not a replacement.** If a task is "set up a Sentry SDK" or "fix/triage/investigate a production issue," that is the official plugin's job (`sentry-sdk-setup`, `sentry-fix-issues`). `sentry-hygiene` does the two things it doesn't: `audit` (validate an existing setup vs docs) and `instrument` (add/fix capture behind the inbound-PII gate).
- **Neither workflow calls a Sentry MCP server.** Both read the repo and the docs only. Do not reach for issue data here; there is no MCP surface.
- **The two PII models are complementary.** The official plugin guards data coming *out* of Sentry (event data is untrusted input, don't leak it into code). `sentry-hygiene` guards the *inbound* half — what the SDK attaches to events on its own (page URL, breadcrumbs, server request data) that no capture call passed.
- **Context7 is optional.** It grounds `audit` in official SDK docs; without it `audit` falls back to `WebFetch` of `docs.sentry.io`, then degrades to structural-only checks and says so in its report.

## Optional

- [Plugin changelog](https://github.com/starfysh-tech/nautilai/blob/main/docs/plugin-changelog.md): Hand-curated log of user-visible plugin changes, newest first.
- [Dual-runtime design](https://github.com/starfysh-tech/nautilai/blob/main/docs/plans/hermes-dual-runtime.md): Why Claude Code and Hermes cannot share one skill directory, and what was verified against each runtime.
- [Dual-runtime conventions](https://github.com/starfysh-tech/nautilai/blob/main/docs/conventions/dual-runtime.md): The rules for keeping a plugin working in both runtimes, and the lessons behind each — including which published Hermes docs turned out to be wrong.
- [Port a plugin to Hermes](https://starfysh-tech.github.io/nautilai/hermes-porting.html): Step-by-step how-to for making a plugin's skill run in Hermes as well as Claude Code — the disqualifiers, the six additive edits, and the verify pass. Human-facing companion to the conventions above.
- [Authoring conventions](https://github.com/starfysh-tech/nautilai/blob/main/docs/conventions/README.md): House rules for plugins in this repo, including the finding-disposition standard every review skill follows.
